steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states ferritic stainless steel contains at least 10.5% chromium and more chromium than austenitic. Since ferritic's minimum is 10.5% and austenitic always has less chromium than ferritic, austenitic must be below 10.5%. The question asks what must be true of both washing machines (200 series, austenitic) and furnaces (300 series, austenitic).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'less than 10.5% chromium': Both appliances use austenitic steel, which the passage establishes has less chromium than ferritic's 10.5% minimum. ✓
  • Choice B — 'more nickel than ferritic': The passage does not support reversing the nickel relationship in this direction. ✗
  • Choice C — 'no cubic crystal structure': The passage does not deny a cubic structure to storage containers or austenitic steel generally. ✗
  • Choice D — 'same nitrogen levels as ferritic': The passage gives no basis for equating nitrogen levels between austenitic and ferritic types. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
